name: ds-tag
description: Small removable chip for a single selected value or keyword, e.g. in a filter bar or multi-select summary.

properties.name: removable
properties.description: Presentational only in this revision; see justification.

constraints.rationale: No primitive references, no raw values.
A non-interactive chip is never a focus stop; contrast must clear AA in both contexts independently.
First registration establishes the API hash; later drift is a rejection.

provenance.author: ai
provenance.justification: Design decision: reused ds-badge's exact color strategy (variant changes foreground only, against emphasis/feedback backdrops that already declare a checked ds.contrastPair) rather than inventing a new palette for tags. Rejected alternative: making `removable` a real interactive affordance that fires a `ds-remove` event and is keyboard-operable, matching how a chip usually behaves in other systems. Rejected for this first registration because keyboard operability requires a dedicated activation-event contract and a Stage 4 keyboard probe target this generic component doesn't implement yet; shipping a boolean that only changes appearance (e.g. shows an 'x' glyph) instead of a real control avoids claiming an interaction contract the component can't yet honor. A follow-up generative revision can add the event once removable tags have a proper archetype.
